meta.title: GitKit — Small Mac apps built on your own git
meta.description: GitKit makes GitFolder and GitKanban: small, local-first macOS apps that use your own git and GitHub. No accounts, no servers — your data stays yours.

hero.title: Small Mac apps, built on your own git.
hero.subtitleHtml: GitKit makes two focused, local-first macOS apps — GitFolder and GitKanban. Both use the git and GitHub setup you already have. No accounts, no servers, no lock-in.

apps.title: Two apps, one idea
apps.subtitle: Keep your work in plain files under git — and get a native Mac app that makes it effortless. Pick the one you need; they share the same principles.

items.name: GitFolder
items.tagline: Automatic version history for the folders on your Mac.
points: Automatic snapshot commits when files change
Uses your existing GitHub + SSH setup
Lives quietly in the macOS menu bar

items.name: GitKanban
items.tagline: A native kanban board whose only data source is a git repo of markdown.
points: Your board is a git repo of markdown files
One file = one card; history comes from git
Agents and scripts can populate it headlessly

principles.title: What every GitKit app shares
principles.subtitle: The same promises hold across the family — because the data is always just your files in your git.

features.title: Local-first & private
features.desc: No GitKit account, no cloud service. Your files move directly between your Mac and the git host you already use.

features.title: Your own git
features.desc: Both apps read and write plain git repositories you own. Nothing is locked into a proprietary format or backend.

features.title: Built on GitHub
features.desc: They lean on the Git and SSH configuration already on your Mac — the same repos, keys, and permissions you trust.

features.title: Native macOS
features.desc: Small, focused, and Mac-native — each app does one thing well and stays out of your way.

cta.title: Own your data. Start with GitFolder.
cta.subtitle: Available on the Mac App Store today — GitKanban is on the way.
